examples.title: Simple Shell Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates basic OS shell concept - reading and executing commands
examples.code:
PROGRAM SimpleShell
BEGIN
  REPEAT
    PRINT "Enter command: "
    INPUT command
    IF command = "exit" THEN
      BREAK
    ELSE
      PARSE command into cmd and args
      IF cmd = "ls" THEN
        CALL ListFiles(args)
      ELSE IF cmd = "cat" THEN
        CALL DisplayFile(args)
      ELSE IF cmd = "mkdir" THEN
        CALL CreateDirectory(args)
      ELSE
        PRINT "Unknown command"
      END IF
    END IF
  UNTIL FALSE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a command-line shell works - it repeatedly accepts user commands, parses them, and calls appropriate OS functions to execute them.

examples.title: Process Creation Example
examples.description: Shows how OS creates and manages new processes using fork()
examples.code:
import os
import sys

def process_example():
    print("Parent process started, PID:", os.getpid())
    
    # Create new process
    pid = os.fork()
    
    if pid == 0:
        # Child process
        print("Child process created, PID:", os.getpid())
        print("Child executing new program...")
        sys.exit(0)
    elif pid > 0:
        # Parent process waits for child
        os.waitpid(pid, 0)
        print("Parent: Child process completed")
    else:
        print("Fork failed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    process_example()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates process creation using fork(). The OS creates a new process by duplicating the parent process. The child gets a return value of 0, while parent gets child's PID.

examples.title: System Call Example
examples.description: Demonstrates use of system calls to create a file and write to it
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates three important system calls: open() creates a file, write() writes data to it, and close() releases the file descriptor. These calls transition from user mode to kernel mode.

examples.title: Memory Allocation Simulation
examples.description: Simulates how OS allocates memory to processes
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MemoryAllocation
BEGIN
  SET totalMemory = 1000  // KB
  SET freeMemory = totalMemory
  SET processList = EMPTY
  
  FUNCTION allocate(processId, size)
  BEGIN
    IF freeMemory >= size THEN
      CREATE memoryBlock WITH address and size
      ADD memoryBlock TO processList
      freeMemory = freeMemory - size
      PRINT "Memory allocated to process", processId
      RETURN memoryBlock
    ELSE
      PRINT "Insufficient memory for process", processId
      RETURN NULL
    END IF
  END FUNCTION
  
  FUNCTION deallocate(processId)
  BEGIN
    FOR EACH block IN processList
      IF block.processId = processId THEN
        freeMemory = freeMemory + block.size
        REMOVE block FROM processList
        PRINT "Memory deallocated from process", processId
        RETURN
      END IF
    END FOR
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates memory management - allocating memory to processes when requested and deallocating when process completes. It tracks free and used memory.
